{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai tyausuyama kohun 1974a.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[World War II]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai tyausuyama kohun 1974a.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[World War II]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sakurai tyausuyama kohun 1974a.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[World War II]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537882]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.511667|N|135.856944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537882]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.511667|N|135.856944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}}

Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb

{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537882]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.511667|N|135.856944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|qid=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}}

A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]][[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537882]] Wikidata name: Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | native_name =桜井茶臼山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.511667|N|135.856944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Sakurai|WD=Q127026}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|30|42|N|135|51|25|E}}}}}} {{nihongo|'''Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun'''|桜井茶臼山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun| Kofun]], located in the Tobiyama neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1980.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2021|title=桜井茶臼山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}} It is thought to have been built in the latter half of the 3rd century, the next generation after [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]. ==Overview== The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is located on a natural hill the left bank of the Hatsuse River and belongs to the Torimiyama Kofun Cluster along with the [[Mesuriyama Kofun]], which has a separate National Historic Site designation. The Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun is 207-meters long with a long and narrow anterior portion and dates from the very early Kofun period. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The existence of this tumulus was discovered only after [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]] as it was covered with scrub and looked like a simple hill. The circular rear portion of the mound is built in three tiers and the rectangular anterior portion in two tiers, and the entire surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones. It has a long, slender anterior section and a handle-shaped mirror-style form. The mound was constructed by shaping the bedrock at the top of the hill, a process known as hill-end cutting. There is no surrounding moat, but a large rectangular area has been created. A rectangular platform with stone paving measuring 9.75 x 12.5 meters on each side is located at the top of the mound, and a square circle of perforated jars (double-rimmed jars) are placed around the base of the platform. Apart from this, there are no traces of special vessel pedestals or cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' as was the case with the Mesuriyama Kofun. The stepped surface is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones.The tumulus is surrounded by a moat. A [[:en:Ground-penetrating radar|Ground-penetrating radar]] survey in 2007 confirmed the existence of an underground stone drainage ditch to drain rainwater that had soaked into the mound.{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} [[Archaeological excavation]]s of the rear circular rear portion were carried out from 1949 to 1950, and it was designated a National Historic Site in 1973. Surveys of the surrounding area were subsequently conducted, and the top of the circular rear part was re-excavated in 2009. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a vertical-entry pit-style stone chamber. It contained a 6.7-meter-long wooden coffin, which has already been looted. The chamber measured about 1.2 meters wide and 1.7 meters high, with walls made of 30-40 cm-wide slabs of stone stacked like bricks, and the ceiling is blocked by 12 huge stones. The entire chamber is painted with [[:en:Cinnabar|Cinnabar]]. The remains of square pillars have been found around the chamber, which are thought to be the remains of a fence. The stones used to make the burial chamber were from Numashima, an island south of [[:en:Awaji Island|Awaji Island]], indicating that the person buried in the tumulus had the authority and wealth to obtain huge stone materials from a considerable distance. Although the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been looted, more than 103 bronze mirror fragments were discovered. Restoration work revealed that these fragments came from more than 81 bronze mirrors, the largest number even found in a tomb in Japan. One of the fragments had [[:en:Chinese characters|Chinese characters]] and measurements matched a triangular-rimmed god-and-beast mirror with an inscription from the first year of the [[:en:Cao Fang|Zhengshi]] era (240), that was excavated from the Kanisawa Kofun in [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. A theory has emerged that this is one of the 100 bronze mirrors bestowed by the [[:en:Cao Fang|Emperor Wei]] to [[Himiko]], as described in the ''[[:en:Wajinden|Wajinden]]''. These factors have let to speculation by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture in 2023 that this was the tomb of an early Yamato king. On the other hand, this tomb and Mesuriyama Kofun are not mentioned historical documents such as the ''[[Kojiki]]'' or ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', and were built in a place relatively far from the [[:en:Makimuku Site|Makimuku ruins]]. 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 木棺.JPG|Wooden coffin excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Tomb
{{small|(Kashihara Archaeological Research Institute Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-1.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 桜井茶臼山古墳出土 二重口縁壺-2.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun
{{small|(Kokugakukan University Museum)}} 茶臼山古墳 (桜井市) 出土二重口縁壺.JPG|Double-rimmed jar excavated from Chausuyama Tomb Kofun
{{small|(Sakurai City Buried Cultural Properties Center)}} The tumulus is about 1.7 kilometers due south from [[:en:Sakurai Station (Nara)|Sakurai Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Osaka Line|Osaka Line]]. ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{Commonscat|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} *[https://sakurai-kankou.jimdoweb.com/名所旧跡/忍坂街道周辺-石位寺ほか/桜井茶臼山古墳/ Sakurai City Tourist Association home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.kashikoken.jp/museum/yamatonoiseki/kofun/2017%20sakuraichausuyama.html Kashihara Institute of Archaeology]{{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:History of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[en:Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Sakurai Chausuyama Kofun}} [[ja:桜井茶臼山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11537882}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
